message:
So last week our power went out for like six hours, and I watched my family group chat absolutely *lose it*. My sister asking where the flashlights are, my mom wondering if the food in the fridge was okay, my brother asking if we needed to evacuate (we didn't, lol), and me just… sitting there realizing I had *no idea* where half our important documents actually were.

It wasn't scary, just chaotic. And kind of funny in hindsight.

But it was also the nudge I needed to finally sit down and think about the basics—you know, where stuff actually lives, what we'd grab if we had to leave, how much water we actually have on hand. Nothing extreme or stressful. Just… knowing.

Turns out when everyone knows the plan, the group chat is a lot calmer next time.

Have you guys ever had one of those "oh wow, we should probably talk about this" moments at home?
